en: Checks whether or not one or more blocks, blockstates or blockdatas can be waterlogged.
Checks if a block is a full block (like dirt, stone, endstone).
Essentially checks if a block is 1x1x1 and only has one mesh.
Checks whether or not one or more blocks, blockstates or blockdatas are waterlogged.
Checks whether the provided blockstates are placed in a world, if not, they are 'virtual' (e. g. on an item).
Some syntaxes will not work if the block state isn't placed in the world.
Checks if the provided chickens are chicken jockeys.
Returns the Angry Property of an entity. (Warden, PigZombie, Wolf, Enderman)
Can be set for all except wardens.
To use this for endermen `Paper 1.18.2+` is required.
Checks if one or more crossbows have any charged projectiles at all.
This behaves the same way the `is charged` condition behaves for crossbows.
Checks if one or more enchanted books have any stored enchantments at all.
Returns whether or not the provided end crystals are showing the bedrock slate underneath them.
Checks if the provided ender signals should drop an item on death. If true, they will drop an item. If false, they will shatter.
Checks if an enderman has been stared at.
Checks if an entity can normally sit.
All the Spawn Categories.
Checks if the provided entities are aggressive.
Some mobs will raise their arm(s) when aggressive:
Drowned, Piglin, Skeleton, Zombie, ZombieVillager, Illusioner, Vindicator, Panda, Pillager, PiglinBrute
Note: This doesn't always return the actual aggressive state as when set, Pandas are always aggressive if their combined Panda Gene is AGGRESSIVE.
Checks if the provided entities being converted.
(Hoglin, Husk, Piglin, Pig Zombie, Skeleton, Zombie, Zombie Villager)
Checks if an entity is dancing.
(Parrot, Allay, Piglin)
Checks whether this entity was spawned from a mob spawner.
Checks if an entity is in powdered snow.
Checks if an entity is leashed.
Checks if an entity is roaring.
For ravagers, Paper 1.19.2+ is required.
Checks if an entity is screaming.
(Goat, Enderman when using Paper 1.18.2+)
Checks whether the provided living entities, blocks, blockstates and items are collidable.
Info:

- LivingEntity: Some entities might be exempted from the collidable rule of this entity. Use the "Entity - Collidable Exemptions" expression to get these. Please note that this method returns only the custom collidable state, not whether the entity is non-collidable for other reasons such as being dead.
Checks if the provided foxes trust the provided offline players.
Checks if goat has the left, right, both or either horn.
Returns whether the item frame is "fixed" or not.
When true it's not possible to destroy/move the frame (e. g. by damage, interaction, pistons, or missing supporting blocks), rotate the item or place/remove items.
Returns whether the item frame is visible or not.
Checks if a jukebox is currently playing a music disc.
Gets whether the provided projectiles have left the hitbox of their shooter and can now hit entities.
This is recalculated each tick if the projectile has a shooter.
Checks if the provided signs can be edited.
Checks if a the provided signs are waxed.
Checks if the item is being dropped from the cursor or the inventory, if the whole stack is being dropped or both.
Closes an anvil gui.
*NOTES*:
- Won't do anything if the anvil gui is not open.
- Must be used before opening another anvil gui for a player.
Opens an anvil GUI to one or more players.
*NOTES*:
- Anvil GUIs are always opened 1 tick later.
- You can only open an anvil GUI for a player if the player is not already viewing another (or this) anvil GUI.

- Using `force` will forcibly close the anvil GUI the player is currently viewing to open a new one. Be aware that, due to how anvil GUIs work, closing an anvil GUI is global, meaning it will be closed for all players viewing it. To avoid this, ensure that you create per-player anvil GUIs.
Prevents an anvil gui from closing.
*NOTES*:
- This effect must be used *BEFORE* the gui is opened; if you need to use it after it's already opened you'll have to open it again.
- If this effect is used you'll need to use the `Anvil GUI - Close` effect to close the gui.
Changes whether one or more blocks or blockstates are waterlogged.
Hides or shows the bedrock slate underneath the provided end crystals.
Sets the location the provided ender signals are moving towards without changing the drop chance and the despawn timer.
Attempts to teleport an enderman to a random nearby location.
Requires Spigot 1.20.1+ or Paper, behavior might be slightly different.
Attempts to teleport an enderman to a random nearby location.
Requires Spigot 1.20.1+.
Makes the provided entities burn (or not) when under the sun.
Creates an entity using this template and spawns it at the provided location.
This is an effect, not a section and can't be used as such.
This effect is heavily influenced by Skript's Spawn effect (EffSecSpawn).
Rotates item frames counter clockwise and clockwise or by specific rotations and a set amount of times.
Sets the item of the provided item frames silently.
Forces a jukebox to eject its disc.
Makes a jukebox start/stop playing.
Attempts to, or forces one or more players to sleep in a bed.
Attempting will fail under vanilla conditions in which the player cannot sleep, ex: Daytime, too far away, etc
Makes a player riptide. (also known as spin attack)
Stopping makes the player riptide for a tick, which stops it a tick later.
The duration must be greater than or equal to 1 tick.
Attack strength will default to 0
`if player is riptiding:` will return true for the given duration.
Gets all the open anvil guis.
Gets one of the 3 items in an anvil gui.
*NOTES*:
- These can be set.
- To apply the changes you need to reopen the anvil gui to the player.
Gets the interactable slots of an Anvil GUI.
Allowed slots: 0, 1 and 2
No slots are interactable by default, resetting or deleting this will make all slots not interactable.
Gets the inventory of one or more anvil guis, this shouldn't be used to edit slots or in non-anvil-gui events.
Creates a new anvil gui. Allows you to copy slots and text data from another anvil gui, this doesn't copy the "prevent closing" property, this is intentional.
Gets the open anvil GUI of one or more players.
Gets the title and the text input of the provided anvil guis.
*NOTES*:
- These can be set.
- To apply the changes you need to reopen the anvil gui to the player.
Gets the anvil gui involved in an event.
All the disabled slots of an armor stand.
Can be set, remove from, added to, reset and deleted.
Returns the Screaming Property of an entity.
This is for Endermen (Requires Paper and 1.18.2+) and Goats.
Returns whether an entity is sat. Can be set.
This works for Cats, Wolves, Parrots, Pandas and Foxes.
On Paper 1.20.1+ it applies for some other entities which have a SITTING pose.
Gets the entity type of the provided entity snapshots.
Gets the provided entities or entity snapshots as their NBT string.
Don't rely on this as the structure of the results can change across versions.
Called when a bat attempts to sleep or wake up from its slumber.
Returns true if the provided blockfaces are aligned with one of the unit axes in 3D Cartesian space (NORTH, SOUTH, EAST, WEST, UP, DOWN).
Checks if a cat is looking up
Checks if a dolphin has a fish.
Checks if an entity is roaring.)
Checks if one or more living entities are awake or sleeping.
Checks if an entity is standing, mainly polar bears, but other entities can be standing.
Returns whether or not the consumable item should be consumed in the Entity Shoot Event.
Checks if a player is in position to inflict a critical hit.
Read https://minecraft.fandom.com/wiki/Damage#Critical_hit for more info.
Checks if the server allows the Nether, the End or Flight
Checks if one or more (thrown) tridents have collided (they must have dealt damage to an entity or have hit the floor).
Checks if one or more (thrown) tridents are enchanted.
Checks if one or more (thrown) tridents are loyal.
Checks if a vindicator is Johnny.
Shows a player the win screen that normally is only displayed after one kills the ender dragon and exits the end for the first time.
Returns the amount of x/y/z to modify to get the represented block(s).
Returns the face relation of a block compared to another block.
If the blocks are not connected nothing will be returned.
Returns the vector corresponding to the provided blockfaces.
Returns the opposite blockface of the provided blockfaces.
Converts shapes into a collection of Bounding Boxes equivalent to the shape they come from.
Returns whether or not a cat is looking up.
Can be set.
Returns the love duration of an animal.
Can be set.
Gets the location where this entity originates from.
This value can be null if the entity hasn't yet been added to the world.
Returns whether or not an entity should burn in the day.
Can be set.
The bow used to fire the arrow and the consumed arrow in the Entity Shoot Event.
Both can be null.
Gets the spawn reason that initially spawned this entity.
The force the arrow was launched with in the Entity Shoot Event.
This number ranges from 0 to 1.
Can be set, reset and deleted.
When set, the new blockdata must be of the same material as the one you're replacing;
when reset and deleted, an empty blockdata of that material will be used.
Various fox properties.
Everything in this expression can be used with `Paper`.
With `Spigot` you can only:
- `Get` and `Set` the Crouching State and Sleeping State
- `Get` the Faceplanted State
Returns whether or not a sign is editable.
Can be set.
Returns loyalty level of a thrown trident.
Allows: Setting, Removing, Adding, Deleting and Resetting.
This value will always be between 0 and 127.
Changing this value won't influence the item itself, just the throw trident.
Get and Set whether 1 or more vindicators are Johnny.
Changing this does not modify the vindicator's name.
Returns the detailed collision shape of a block.
Returns the entity at which this warden is most angry.
Runs the code inside of it when the provided block gets broken.
Local Variables that are:
- defined BEFORE this section CAN be used inside of it.
- defined AFTER this section CANNOT be used inside of it.
- defined INSIDE this section CANNOT be used outside of it.
The Rarity of an item.
COMMON = White
UNCOMMON = Yellow
RARE = Aqua
EPIC = Light Purple
A Voxel Shape, usually used for blocks such as Stairs.
You can store those but it's not persistent across restarts.
Runs the code inside of it when the provided entity dies.
Local Variables that are:
- defined BEFORE this section CAN be used inside of it.
- defined AFTER this section CANNOT be used inside of it.
- defined INSIDE this section CANNOT be used outside of it.
Runs the code inside of it when the provided entity jumps.
Only works for horses on Spigot, Paper is required for players and entities.
Local Variables that are:
- defined BEFORE this section CAN be used inside of it.
- defined AFTER this section CANNOT be used inside of it.
- defined INSIDE this section CANNOT be used outside of it.
Gets the base amount of damage one or more arrows will do.
Defaults to 2.0 for a normal arrow with 0.5 * (1 + power level) added for arrows fired from enchanted bows.
Can be set, added to, removed from, deleted (set to 0) and reset (set to 2, the default value).
Sets the number of times this arrow can pierce through an entity. Must be between 0 and 127.
Gets a list or one of the coordinates of:
- Locations
- Vectors
- Chunks (only X and Z)
- EulerAngles
- 2D Vectors (only X and Z)
- 3D Vectors
- 4D Vectors (XYZW)
- Quaternions (XYZW)
- AxisAngles (XYZW)
- MapCursor
Note: Out of all of these Lusk only implements EulerAngles.
Returns the time or ticks since one or more bells have been resonating, or 0 seconds for each bell that's not currently resonating.
A bell will typically resonate for 40 ticks (2 seconds)
Note: if you wish to use this expression on Skript 2.9+ you will need to use Lusk 1.3+.
Returns the time or ticks since one or more bells have been shaking, or 0 seconds for each bell that's not currently resonating.
A bell will typically resonate for 50 ticks (2.5 seconds)
Note: if you wish to use this expression on Skript 2.9+ you will need to use Lusk 1.3+.
Returns a value that represents how 'slippery' the block is.
Blocks with higher slipperiness, like ice can be slid on further by the player and other entities.
Most blocks have a default slipperiness of 0.6.
Can be used with items, blocks, blockstates, and blockdatas.
Gets either the lesser or the greater corner or the lesser of one or more bounding boxes.
Returns vectors.
Returns whether or not one or more blocks, blockstates or blockdatas are waterlogged.
Can be set.
Gets all the entities within a bounding box in a specific world.
Gets the x,y or z coordinate for the lesser corner, the greater corner or the center of one or more bounding boxes.
Gets the height, X width and Z width of 1 or more bounding boxes.
Creates a new empty Bounding Box, one from two corners or a copy of a given bounding box
Gets the volume of 1 or more bounding boxes.
Returns the bounding box of a block or an entity which reflects its location and size. (But not its world, kind of like vectors)
Returns whether or not a cat is lying down.
Can be set and reset.
Called when an equippable item is dispensed from a block and equipped on a nearby entity.
Called when a bell is rung.
Checks if the provided chickens are chicken jockeys.
Returns the first, second, or both trusted players of 1 or more foxes.
*NOTES*:
- The first and second trusted players can both be set to a single `offline player`, the trusted players can be set to 2 offline players (or 1, which only sets the first one).
- The second trusted player must be set after the first one, which also means you can't clear the first one if the second one is set, both actions will fail silently.
- If you wish to set/clear both, do so by using the second pattern: `clear trusted players of {_fox}`
This event is called when a player takes items out of the furnace.
This event requires Paper.
Fired after a player has respawned.
This event requires Paper.
Called when an item is about to be composted by an entity.
This event requires Paper.

Called when the player tries to attack an entity. This occurs before any of the damage logic, so cancelling this event will prevent any sort of sounds from being played when attacking. This event will fire as cancelled for certain entities, use the "will be damaged" condition to check if the entity will not actually be attacked.
Note: there may be other factors (invulnerability, etc) that will prevent this entity from being attacked that this event will not cover.
This event requires Paper.
Called when an item is about to be composted by a hopper.
To prevent hoppers from moving items into composters, cancel the Inventory move event from SkBee.
Called when a player changes their main hand in the client settings.
Called when a Furnace starts smelting.
This Condition requires Paper.
Checks whether or not the composter's level will rise in a Compost Item Event
This Condition requires Paper.
Checks whether or not the Slot Change event should trigger advancements.
This Condition requires Paper.
Checks whether or not the entity in the Pre Damage Event will be damaged.
Checks if an entity is an NPC. (Citizens)
Checks if the fox is defending.
Checks if the fox is faceplanted.
Checks if a fox is leaping.
Checks if a player is crawling.
Deprecated since 2.7: https://docs.skriptlang.org/nightly/master/docs.html?search=#CondRespawnLocation
This Condition requires Paper.
Checks whether or not the player in the Post Respawn event has respawned at they bed.
This event requires Paper.
Called when a Warden's anger level has changed due to another entity.
If the event is cancelled, the warden's anger level will not change.
Checks if a fox is crouching.
This Condition requires Paper.
Checks whether or not the item is being placed inside the flower pot in a Flower Pot Manipulate Event.
This Effect requires Paper.
Can only be used in a Compost Item Event.
Sets whether or not the composter's level should rise.
Returns the best Slot for an item.
Returns the color of an item's rarity.
Can be used with the item itself and it will get its rarity's color without getting the rarity itself if you don't need it.
Returns all the equipment slots.
Gets whether the provided foxed are defending, can be set and reset.
Shows a player the win screen that normally is only displayed after one kills the ender dragon and exits the end for the first time.
Returns the Maximum Vanilla Level for an Enchantment.
Returns the rarity of an item.
Can be set.
Before Lusk 1.2 (and Minecraft 1.20.5), this expression returned strings and also worked for enchantments, due to some major changes enchantments no longer have a rarity.
Obtains the blast resistance value (also known as block "durability").
This value is used in explosions to calculate whether a block should be broken or not.
Only works for placeable item/blocks.
Returns the applied color in the Dye event.
This expression can be set to another dye color.
Returns the Minimum Vanilla Level for an Enchantment.
Gets the pattern type selected in a Loom Pattern Select event
Gets a version from a string.
Gets whether the provided foxes are faceplanted, if running Paper this can be set and reset.
Gets a copy of the item in the provided item frames.
Can be set and deleted, this expression supports the Silent Section, see examples.
The chance of the item being dropped upon this item frame's destruction.
Can be set, removed from, added to, reset (1) and deleted (0).
The final value is always clamped between 0 and 1.
1 = always drops; 0 = never drops.
Returns the anger of a warden.
Angry = 80 or above
Agitated = 40-79
Calm = 0-39
Use the respective conditions to check for those 3 states.
Gets whether the provided foxed are leaping, can be set and reset.
Returns the Enchantments this item can normally have.
Returns the music disc within a jukebox.
Can be set, reset and deleted.
Returns the provided llamas' strength.
A higher strength llama will have more inventory slots and be more threatening to entities.
Can be set.
Must be between 1 and 5.
Gets whether the item frame is visible or not.
Can be set.
Returns the previous gamemode of a player.
The fuse ticks of a Creeper, Primed TNT or TNT Minecart, this differs based on which one of these 3 is used, read below.
Can be set, added to, removed from and reset (which sets it to 0, this can or cannot be what you want based on the entity, read below).
The `max` keyword only applies to creepers.
`creeper`:
- the time that the creeper has been in the primed state for;
- if max is used it will return the maximum amount of time that the creeper can stay in the ignited state for until it explodes;
- both math and regular fuse time must be greater than 0, regular fuse time cannot be greater than max fuse time;
`primed tnt`: the time until the primed tnt explodes;
`tnt minecart`: the time until the minecart explodes, -1 if not ignited.
Returns the Projectile Items charged in the provided crossbows.
Can be set, added to, removed from, reset and deleted.
Returns the Enchantments stored in an enchanted book.
Can be set, added to, removed from, reset and deleted.
Gets the location that the provided end crystals are pointing their beam to.
Can be set, if the provided location is in a different world it will fail silently.
Gets how long the provided ender signals have been alive for.
Can be set.
Either as ticks or a timespan.
When greater than 80 ticks (or 4 seconds), it will despawn on the next tick.
Called when a Brewing Stand brews.
Called when a bell resonated after being rung and highlights nearby raiders.
A bell will only resonate if raiders are in the vicinity of the bell.
Gets the item the provided ender signals will display and drop on death.
Checks if an item can be enchanted with an Enchantment.
This does not check if the enchantment conflicts with any enchantments already applied on the item.
Checks if an allay can be duplicated.
Checks if an enderman has been looked at.
Checks if an entity is angry.
(Warden, PigZombie, Wolf, Enderman)
Checks if an entity is in powdered snow.
Checks if an entity is screaming.
(Enderman, Goat)
This event requires Paper.
Fired when an Enderman determines if it should attack a player or not.
Starts off cancelled if the player is wearing a pumpkin head or is not looking at the Enderman, according to Vanilla rules.
Called when an EnderDragon switches phase.
This event requires Paper.
Called when a block is about to dispense an item.
Called when an entity enters love mode.
This can be cancelled but the item will still be consumed that was used to make the entity enter into love mode.
This Event requires Paper.
Called when a player attempts to sleep but fails..
Called when an entity changes its pose.
Called when an ElderGuardian appears in front of a Player.
This event requires Paper.
Called when a block tries to dispense an item, but its inventory is empty.
Checks if an ocelot trusts players.
Checks if a Warden is agitated.
Checks if a Warden is calm.
Checks if an entity can normally sit.
This Effect requires Paper.
Can only be used in a Slot Change Event.
Sets whether or not the event should trigger advancements.
Wakes a player/villager up.
Checks whether the server has a whitelist or not.
Converts Villagers into Zombie Villagers as if they were killed by a Zombie.
Forces an allay to duplicate itself without dance or item necessary.
Sets whether the fuel of the brewing stand in the Brewing Stand Fuel Event will be reduced / consumed.
Checks whether or not the brewing stand's fuel will be consumed in a Brewing Stand Fuel Event
Checks if an entity is underwater.
Forces an Allay to start/stop dancing.
Checks whether this entity was spawned from a mob spawner.
Sets if the whitelist is enforced.
Attempts to teleport an enderman to a random nearby location.
Checks whether the whitelist is enforced. If the whitelist is enforced, non-whitelisted players will be disconnected when the server whitelist is reloaded.
This Condition requires Paper.
Checks whether or not the worldborder change is instant in the worldborder change event
Makes a warden sense a disturbance at the provided location.
Sets if the server is whitelisted.
Does nothing.
Useful as a placeholder.
Gets the location the provided ender signals are moving towards.
When set, the drop chance resets to a random value and the despawn timer gets set back to 0.
Returns whether or not an entity is interested.
(Wolf, Fox)
Gets the jukebox the allay is set to dance to.
Returns the Ender Dragon phase in an Ender Dragon Phase Change Event.
Can be set. (Setting will always change the NEW phase)
Gets the location where this entity originates from.
This value can be null if the entity hasn't yet been added to the world.
Returns the Screaming State of an entity.
(Enderman, Goat)
Returns the fuel power for the fuel in the Brewing Stand Fuel event.
Can be set.
Returns the brewing fuel level of a Brewing Stand.
Can be set, reset and deleted.
Returns the brewing ingredient of a Brewing Stand.
Can be set.
Returns the brewing fuel item of a Brewing Stand.
Can be set.
Returns the rotation of an Item Frame.
Returns the Angry State of an entity.
(Warden, PigZombie, Wolf, Enderman)
Can be set for all except wardens.
Gets the entity which pushed the affected entity in a Damage Push event
Returns the entity at which this warden is most angry.
Returns the past/future anger of the warden in the Warden Anger Change event.
The future anger level can be set.
Returns the new/old anger of the warden in the World Border Change events.
Can be set only within the Start variant of the event.
Make a villager shake his head.
Returns the location in which the player has last died.
Returns the category of spawn to which this entity belongs.
Gets the spawn reason that initially spawned this entity.
Returns one or more items without enchantments.
Called when a Cauldron's level changes.'
Returns the love duration of an animal.
Can be set.
Returns the previous gamemode of a player.
Returns whether or not an ocelot trusts players.
Can be set.
Returns the Held Block Data of the provided Endermen.
Returns a value that represents how 'slippery' the block is. Blocks with higher slipperiness, like ice can be slid on further by the player and other entities.
Most blocks have a default slipperiness of 0.6.
Only works for placeable item/blocks.
Returns the Sound this entity makes when splashing in water. For most entities, this is just 'ENTITY_GENERIC_SPLASH'.
Returns the past/future center of the world border in the World Border Change events.
The future center can be set.
Checks if a living entity is climbing (Ladders, vines, spiders etc).
Checks if an entity is being converted.
(Hoglin, Husk, Piglin, Pig Zombie, Skeleton, Zombie, Zombie Villager)
This event requires Paper.
Called when an enderman escapes.
CRITICAL_HIT: The enderman has teleported away due to a critical hit
DROWN: Specific case for CRITICAL_HIT where the enderman is taking rain damage
INDIRECT: The enderman has teleported away due to indirect damage (ranged)
RUNAWAY: The enderman has stopped attacking and ran away
STARE: The enderman has teleported away due to the player staring at it during combat
Returns the Sound this entity makes while swimming.
Checks if a crossbow, creeper, wither or wither skull is charged.
Checks if a llama is in a caravan.
Checks if an horse is eating.
Checks if an horse is eating grass.
Checks if an horse is rearing.
Checks if a jukebox contains a music disc.
Checks if a sign can be edited.
Checks if a skeleton horse is trapped.
Makes a goat ram an entity.
Makes a llama join another llama's caravan.
Makes a jukebox start/stop playing.
Checks if a jukebox is currently playing a music disc.
Checks if another llama is currently following behind this llama.
Checks if a player is in position to inflict a critical hit.
Read https://minecraft.fandom.com/wiki/Damage#Critical_hit for more info.
Checks if the string is a valid version
Forces a jukebox to eject its disc.
The rotation of each part of an armor stand.
Can be set, added to, removed from, and reset.
All change modes allow both a vector and an euler angle.
Makes a llama leave its caravan.
Checks if an iron golem was created by a player.
Returns whether or not an horse is eating.
Can be set and reset.
Returns the command within a command block.
Can be set.
Works with minecart with command block.
Returns the domestication level and the maximum domestication level of an horse.
Can be set.
Returns whether or not a cat is lying down.
Can be set.
Gets the treasure location the provided dolphins try to guide players to.
This value is calculated if the player has fed the dolphin a fish (which Lusk includes), `DOLPHIN_SWIM_TO_TREASURE` goal.
Can be set, but it only has an effect if the dolphin is currently leading a player, as this value is recalculated next time it leads a player.
The world of the location you give it does not matter, as the dolphin will always use the world it is currently in.
Gets the displayed item of the provided fireballs.
Can be set.
Returns the caravan head of a llama.
Returns the caravan tail of a llama.
Returns the time till a chicken lays an egg.
Returns whether or not a goat has either horn.
Can be set and reset.
Returns whether or not an horse is eating grass.
Can be set and reset.
Returns the jump strength of an horse.
Can be set.
Returns the music disc within a jukebox.
Can be set, reset and deleted.
Fired when an entity is pushed by another entity's attack.
The acceleration vector can be modified. If this event is cancelled, the entity will not get pushed.
Note: Some entities might trigger this multiple times on the same entity as multiple acceleration calculations are done.
Returns the trap time of the skeleton horse.
Gets the item the provided ender signals will display and drop on death.
Gets whether or not the entity's custom name is displayed client side.
This value has no effect on players, they will always display their name.
The server version.
Gets whether the provided projectiles have left the hitbox of their shooter and can now hit entities.
This is recalculated each tick if the projectile has a shooter.
Can be set.
Returns whether or not the provided signs are waxed.
Can be set and reset (which makes it not waxed).
The command used in an effect command event.
This doesn't include the prefix (usually `!`).
Can be set, reset and deleted.
Returns the given string with the small font.
'Lenient' allows uppercase characters to not be included.
Retrieves the used hand or item used in events where either hand is used.
The first pattern returns the Slot (Item + Index) of the item in the used hand.
- Note: using this is not the same as using `event-slot` or `event-item`, if either is available, use that
The second pattern returns an Equipment Slot that's either `hand_slot` or `off_hand_slot`.
Supported Events:
- on Right Click
- on Item Consume
- on Arm Swing
- on Entity Shoot (different than 'on Shoot'!)
- on Crossbow Load
- on Place (includes 'on Block Place', 'on Hanging Place', and 'on Entity Place')
- on Resurrect
- on Player Fish
- on Bucket Fill
- on Bucket Empty
- on Elytra Boost
- on Player Harvest
- on Can Build Check
- on Player Shear Entity
- on Player Leash Entity
- on Player Unleash Entity
- on Player Use Unknown Entity
Returns the duration of the change in the World Border Start Change event.
Can be set.
Requires Paper.
Can be set.
The velocity vector in the Player Velocity Change Event, can be set, deleted and reset.
A Banner Pattern.
Runs the code inside of it when the provided entity takes damage.
Local Variables that are:
- defined BEFORE this section CAN be used inside of it.
- defined AFTER this section CANNOT be used inside of it.
- defined INSIDE this section CANNOT be used outside of it.
Executes the effects within this section without making sounds, this currently only supports the changing of an item frame's item.
Note: this is not related to errors or warnings in the console.
Gets the list of entities (or their UUIDs) which are exempt from the provided entities' collidable rule and whose collision with this entity will behave the opposite of it.
Can be set, added to, removed from, and deleted.
For example if collidable is true and an entity is in the exemptions then it will not collide with it.
Similarly if collidable is false and an entity is in this set then it will still collide with it.
Note: **these exemptions are not (currently) persistent.**
Note: **the client may predict the collision between itself and another entity, resulting in those exemptions not being accurate for player collisions.**
**This expression should therefore only be used to exempt non-player entities.**
**To exempt collisions for a player, use the Team Collision Rule Option in combination with a Scoreboard and a Team.**
Returns the Sound this entity makes when splashing in water at high speeds. For most entities, this is just 'ENTITY_GENERIC_SPLASH'.
Gets the item in one or more equipment slots of one or more entities, if the entity cannot use a provided slot it will fail silently.
This might not work when trying to get a slot on an entity that doesnt have that slot AND if you're not running at least Paper 1.21
Can be set.
Returns the network protocol ID for one or more entities.
Creates an EntitySnapshot representing the current state of this entity.
Returns the category of spawn to which this entity belongs.
Called when a player interacts with an armor stand and will either swap, retrieve, place an item.
`event-equipmentslot` is the clicked slot of the armor stand, to get the hand use the Used Hand expression
`event-entity` is the armor stand
`past event-item` is the item the armor stand is holding
`future event-item` (or `event-item`) is the item the player is holding
`event-armorstand interaction` is the type of manipulation that's happening during the event
(NOTE: this will only ever return `Place`, `Retrieve` or `Change`)
**THIS IS AN EXTENDED VERSION OF SKRIPT'S IGNITE EVENT.**
`event-player` = the Player that placed/ignited the fire block, or not set if not ignited by a Player.
`event-entity` = the Entity that placed/ignited the fire block, or not set if not ignited by an Entity.
`event-ignitecause` = the value detailing the cause of the block ignition.
Called when a block is ignited. If you want to catch when a Player places fire, use the place event.
If this event is cancelled, the block will not be ignited.
Returns the Aggressive Property of an entity.
Can be set.
Some mobs will raise their arm(s) when aggressive:
Drowned, Piglin, Skeleton, Zombie, ZombieVillager, Illusioner, Vindicator, Panda, Pillager, PiglinBrute
Note: This doesn't always return the actual aggressive state as when set, Pandas are always aggressive if their combined Panda Gene is AGGRESSIVE.
Called when an ItemFrame is having an item rotated, added, or removed from it.
Checks if the provided foxed or wolves are interested.
For Foxes, Paper 1.18.2+ is required.
Checks if an entity is sitting.
(Camels, Cats, Wolves, Parrots, Pandas and Foxes)
Checks if an anvil gui can be closed.
This is only true if the `Anvil GUI - Prevent Closing` effect is used and cannot be reversed without creating a new gui.
Checks if a player is currently viewing a specific anvil GUI or any at all.
Checks if a specific equipment slot of an armorstand is disabled.
Called when the player is attempting to rename a mob.
Called when a LivingEntity loads a crossbow with a projectile.
Called when a player changes a block by right clicking it with a shovel.
Called when a player changes a block by right clicking it with an axe.
Called when a player changes a block by right clicking it with a hoe.
This Event requires Paper.

Checks whether or not the dropped item in the Pickup Attempt Event will fly at the player.
Rings a bell.
The blockface must be cartesian.

Searched Block expression for the Hopper Inventory Search Event.

The hand used in events where you might need to distinguish between the main and offhand.
Checks if a bell is resonating.

Returns the brewing time of a Brewing Stand (the time before the brewing is over, 0 seconds = finished, 20 seconds = just started. Can be set to a longer time, progress won't be displayed until it reaches 20 seconds).

Represents all the available banner pattern types.
All the Block Actions.
Represents a captured state of a block, which will not change automatically.

Unlike Block, which only one object can exist per coordinate, BlockState can exist multiple times for any given Block. Note that another plugin may change the state of the block and you will not know, or they may change the block to another type entirely, causing your BlockState to become invalid.
All the Body Parts.
Represents an immutable copy of an entity's state. Can be used at any time to create an instance of the stored entity.
Requires 1.20.5 to be stored and parsed.
EulerAngle is used to represent 3 angles, one for each axis (x, y, z). The angles are in radians
All the Ignition Causes.

Rotations are used in item frames.

This works for leather armor, horse leather armor and wolf armor.
This might not work correctly on Spigot.
Whether or not one or more bounding boxes overlap other bounding boxes.
Bounding boxes that are only intersecting at the borders are not considered overlapping.
Whether or not a Vector, Location, or Bounding Box is within a Bounding Box.
Vector and Location = Checks if the Bounding Box contains a specified position.
Bounding Box = Checks if the Bounding Box fully contains a Bounding Box.

Gets and sets the `canMove` property of an armorstand entity or item.
Unlike other Armorstand properties, this one cannot be used on the armorstand item as of 1.21.3.

Gets and sets the `hasBasePlate` property of an armorstand entity or item, to do so with an armorstand item you must be using Paper.
Represents the maximum, minimum or current amount of fluid contained within a block, either by itself or inside a cauldron.
Minimum Level requires Paper and 1.18.2.
This expression includes liquid blocks, cauldron and composters.
For water and lava blocks the levels have special meanings: a level of 0 corresponds to a source block, 1-7 regular fluid heights, and 8-15 to "falling" fluids.
All falling fluids have the same behaviour, but the level corresponds to that of the block above them, equal to this. level - 8
**Note that counterintuitively, an adjusted level of 1 is the highest level, whilst 7 is the lowest.**
May not be higher than the max level.
Can be set, added to, removed from and reset.
Reset requires Paper and 1.18.2.
Gets the String NBT of the provided entity snapshots.
Attempts to create an entity snapshots from a previously created string nbt of one.

Can bet set.

Can be: set, added to, removed from, removed all x from, cleared and reset.
Clear and reset do the same thing, same for remove and remove all.
Remove and remove all can take either potion effects or potion effect types.

When true it's not possible to destroy/move the frame (e. g. by damage, interaction, pistons, or missing supporting blocks), rotate the item or place/remove items.

Setting this modifies the order for the player you're setting it for.
Called when an Anvil Gui is clicked.
Called when an Anvil Gui is opened.
*Note*:
- To cancel this event you'll need to use the `Anvil GUI - Prevent Closing` effect before this event is called.
Called when an Anvil Gui is opened.
Can be cancelled.
Called when the progress of a block break is updated.
`event-number` = the block damage progress, ranges from 0.0 to 1.0, where 0 is no damage and 1.0 is the most damage.
Called when the fluid level of a block changes due to changes in adjacent blocks.
Called when the brewing of the contents inside a Brewing Stand is complete.
Called when a brewing stand starts to brew.
Called when an ItemStack is about to increase the fuel level of a brewing stand.
Called when a Campfire starts to cook.
Called when a Cauldron's level changes.
This event requires Paper.
Called when an item is about to be composted by an entity.
This event requires Paper.
Called when an item is about to be composted by a hopper.
To prevent hoppers from moving items into composters, cancel the Inventory move event from SkBee.
Called when an equipable item is dispensed from a block and equipped on a nearby entity.
Called when a block tries to dispense an item, but its inventory is empty.
Called when a block is about to dispense an item.
Called when an ElderGuardian appears in front of a Player.
Called when an EnderDragon changes phase.
Fired when an Enderman determines if it should attack a player or not.
Starts off cancelled if the player is wearing a pumpkin head or is not looking at the Enderman, according to Vanilla rules.
Called when an enderman escapes.
CRITICAL_HIT: The enderman has teleported away due to a critical hit
DROWN: Specific case for CRITICAL_HIT where the enderman is taking rain damage
INDIRECT: The enderman has teleported away due to indirect damage (ranged)
RUNAWAY: The enderman has stopped attacking and ran away
STARE: The enderman has teleported away due to the player staring at it during combat

Called when an entity enters the hitbox of a block. Only called for blocks that react when an entity is inside. If cancelled, any action that would have resulted from that entity being in the block will not happen (such as extinguishing an entity in a cauldron).
Blocks this is currently called for:
Big dripleaf
Bubble column
Buttons
Cactus
Campfire
Cauldron
Crops
Ender Portal
Fires
Frogspawn
Honey
Hopper
Detector rails
Nether portals
Powdered snow
Pressure plates
Sweet berry bush
Tripwire
Waterlily
Web
Wither rose
Called when an entity receives knockback.
Fired when an Entity is knocked back by the hit of another Entity.
The acceleration vector can be modified. If this event is cancelled, the entity is not knocked back.
Called when an entity enters love mode.
This can be cancelled but the used item will still be consumed.
Triggered when an entity is created in the world by a player "placing" an item on a block.
Note that this event is currently only fired for four specific placements: armor stands, boats, minecarts, and end crystals.
Called when an entity changes its pose.
Note: **`past event-pose` is the same as `pose of event-entity`, due to this it's not guaranteed that it will always work correctly.**
Fired when an entity is pushed by another entity's attack.
The acceleration vector can be modified. If this event is cancelled, the entity will not get pushed.
Note: Some entities might trigger this multiple times on the same entity as multiple acceleration calculations are done.
Called when a LivingEntity shoots a bow firing an arrow.
This event is called when a player takes items out of the furnace.
event-integer -> item amount being retrieved
Called when a Furnace starts smelting, or cooking for that matter.
Called when an item is put in a slot for repair or unenchanting in a grindstone.
This event is called whenever a player harvests a block.
A 'harvest' is when a block drops an item (usually some sort of crop) and changes state, but is not broken in order to drop the item.
This event is not called for when a block is broken.
Called when a player interacts with a block or entity by clicking.
This event is always called unlike Skript's click event, meaning that it can be called for both hands.
You can use `main hand` and `off hand` to make it per hand.
`event-equipmentslot` = the slot of the used hand
`event-blockaction` = the action of this event
`event-vector` = the vector from the player to the interaction point
`event-location` = the location of the interaction point
Called when a player drops an item from an inventory (or their own).
Called when a player changes their main hand in the client settings.
Called when a player attempts to pick an item up from the ground.
Fired after a player has respawned.

Called when the player tries to attack an entity. This occurs before any of the damage logic, so cancelling this event will prevent any sort of sounds from being played when attacking. This event will fire as cancelled for certain entities, use the "will be damaged" condition to check if the entity will not actually be attacked.
Note: there may be other factors (invulnerability, etc) that will prevent this entity from being attacked that this event will not cover.
Called when a player attempts to sleep but fails..
Called when the velocity of a player changes due to outside circumstances.
Called when someone uses a skript effect through the chat, also known as effect command, this can be enabled through skript's config.
Called when the recipe of an Item is completed inside a smithing table.
Called when a Tameable dies and sends a death message.
Called when a Warden's anger level has changed due to another entity.
If the event is cancelled, the warden's anger level will not change.
Fires when the server needs to verify if a player is whitelisted. Plugins may override/control the servers whitelist with this event, and dynamically change the kick message.
An anvil gui.
